companies.name: Aecon Group
companies.notes: Involved in 14 contracts across transit, nuclear, and highways totalling $70.3B in combined contract value. Actual Aecon share is a fraction of this — they are one of multiple consortium members on every contract. Known specific amounts: ~$1.3B (Darlington SMR construction share), $1.3B (55% of Shoreline Power $1.3B fuel channel contract).

public_accounts_ministries.ministry: Ministry Of Transportation
Ministry Of Public And Business Service Delivery
Ministry Of Natural Resources And Forestry

companies.name: EllisDon
companies.notes: Lead builder (with PCL) on 4 Ontario hospital projects totalling $22.3B in contract value. Actual EllisDon share not publicly disclosed — contracts are JVs with PCL and other partners. EllisDon is likely receiving 40-50% of the construction portion.

public_accounts_ministries.ministry: Ministry Of Transportation

companies.name: PCL Construction
companies.notes: Lead builder (with EllisDon) on 5 Ontario hospital projects totalling $22.0B in contract value. Actual PCL share not publicly disclosed — contracts are JVs with EllisDon and other partners.

companies.name: Webuild Group
companies.notes: Member of Connect 6ix ($9B Ontario Line RSSOM), Mobilinx ($4.6B Hazel McCallion LRT), and Pape North Connect (Ontario Line Pape). Actual shares not publicly disclosed.

companies.name: Hitachi Rail
companies.notes: Member of Connect 6ix ($9B Ontario Line RSSOM) and Mobilinx ($4.6B Hazel McCallion LRT). Actual share of each consortium not publicly disclosed.

companies.name: Ferrovial Construction Canada
companies.notes: Lead construction partner in Ontario Transit Group ($6B Ontario Line South). Actual share not publicly disclosed.

Ministry Of Solicitor General

companies.name: VINCI Construction
companies.notes: Partner in Ontario Transit Group ($6B Ontario Line South) via VINCI Construction Grands Projets. Actual share not publicly disclosed.

companies.name: FCC Canada (FCC Construccion)
companies.notes: Member of multiple transit consortia: Scarborough Transit Connect ($5.7B SRS), North End Connectors ($1.4B YNSE), GO ONxpress ($1.6B terminated). Actual shares not publicly disclosed.

companies.name: Ghella Canada
companies.notes: Italian tunnelling specialist. Member of West End Connectors (20% share of $729M ECWE tunnel) and North End Connectors (share of $1.4B YNSE tunnel). Actual Ghella share not publicly disclosed. No political connections found — won contracts through competitive IO/Metrolinx procurement.

companies.notes: Will operate Ontario Line for 30 years as part of Connect 6ix ($9B). Also in Mobilinx consortium ($4.6B). Actual share not publicly disclosed.

companies.notes: Equity provider in multiple P3 consortia (Mississauga Hospital $13.9B, Ontario Line $9B). Does not receive full contract values — provides financing capital.

companies.name: Therme Group
companies.notes: Austrian spa company; 95-year lease on Ontario Place West Island; NYT found 5 of 6 claimed spas were NOT Therme's; AG found total project cost $2.2B (up from $335-424M); Ford met CEO personally June 2022

companies.name: Live Nation
companies.notes: Expanding Budweiser Stage to 20K capacity; 30+15yr lease at Ontario Place

companies.name: Nieuport Aviation (J.P. Morgan)
companies.notes: Billy Bishop terminal owner; 100% J.P. Morgan Asset Management; retained Ford's former deputy chief of staff Mark Lawson as lobbyist

companies.name: Porter Airlines
companies.notes: Robert Deluce (Exec Chair) has deep Conservative connections; OMERS owns 21.4%; stands to benefit massively from Billy Bishop expansion Note: Porter Airlines itself is not a direct government contract recipient. The political connections run through Nieuport Aviation (terminal owner) and PortsToronto (board). Porter would benefit indirectly from Billy Bishop expansion.

companies.name: Ontario Science Partners
companies.notes: Includes Hariri Pontarini Architects; $1.04B fixed-price DBFM contract for new Science Centre at Ontario Place

companies.name: StrategyCorp
companies.notes: Hired by Therme in 2018 (under Liberals); continued lobbying after Ford won; 3 principals registered as consultant lobbyists for Therme; founded by Leslie Noble (Harris campaign manager)
